questions.question: According to the text, why does Monday often feel like a difficult transition?
questions.answer: Monday feels abrupt because it’s the shift from the relaxed leisure of the weekend to the demands of work.

questions.question: Which deities are associated with Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday, respectively?
questions.answer: Tuesday - Tyr, Wednesday - Odin, Thursday - Thor, Friday - Jupiter.

questions.question: What generally happens on Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday in business cultures?
questions.answer: Productivity peaks and meetings are common on these days.

questions.question: What does Saturday's name, derived from Saturn, traditionally represent?
questions.answer: Saturday is typically devoted to recreation and socialising.

questions.question: How does Sunday traditionally function, according to the text?
questions.answer: Sunday traditionally offers rest or reflection, though some experience anxiety about the upcoming Monday.

questions.question: What does the text suggest about the relationship between the days of the week and our emotions?
questions.answer: The days form a narrative loop that influences our emotions, routines, and sense of control.

questions.question: Why does the text describe the phenomenon of ‘weekend anxiety’ as ‘powerful’?
questions.answer: It highlights how ingrained cultural labels associated with the days of the week have become in our lives.
